subtasks.description: Query Datadog license allocation data including license type, quantity allocated, cost per license, and assignment dates
Query Datadog actual usage metrics including daily active users, metrics ingestion volume, log ingestion volume, host count, container count, and time-series data over the period
Calculate growth rates, utilization ratios (actual usage vs allocated licenses), trend analysis with month-over-month and year-over-year growth, and identify peak usage periods and patterns

plan.cost_rationale: Split into two separate SQL queries (license allocation and usage metrics) because they query different data sources/tables and return distinct result sets that are then reconciled. The cross_recon subtask (mid tier) performs the analysis: it calculates utilization ratios, growth rates, trend analysis, and compares allocations against actual usage to identify gaps and patterns. This is a moderate-complexity reconciliation task requiring inference and comparative analysis. We avoid a multitool_plan because no enterprise tool registry calls are requested—this is pure data analysis. We avoid separate policy_action because no compliance/governance decisions are requested. Three subtasks minimum: two independent data retrieval tasks plus one analysis task that depends on both.

output.sql: SELECT product, event_type, event_date, SUM(amount_usd) AS total_cost, SUM(daily_active_seats) AS total_seats, COUNT(DISTINCT license_id) AS license_count, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS active_users FROM consumption_facts WHERE publisher = 'Datadog' AND event_date >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY product, event_type, event_date ORDER BY event_date DESC, product

SELECT event_date, product, SUM(daily_active_seats) AS daily_active_users, SUM(amount_usd) AS usage_cost, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS unique_users, COUNT(*) AS event_count FROM consumption_facts WHERE publisher = 'Datadog' AND event_date >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY event_date, product ORDER BY event_date, product
